title: encrypting your data
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"t96u","text":"How to encrypt a folder or a file using the command line on a linux/mac system !","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In order to encrypt your data, you'll need to zip your folder or file, creating a password protected archive. People without the password will still be able to look at the names of the files in the archive, but won't be able to read the file. Using the command line, navigate to the folder you want to encrypt and run: zip -re output_archive.zip my_folder with "output_archive" the name of the encrypted archive you want to create and "my_folder" the name of the folder (or file) you want to encrypt. In the prompt, a password will be aseked twice. Make sure to use a strong password (8-10 characters with special characters)
To decrypt the archive, use the unzip command as usual. A prompt will ask you the password before unzipping the files. unzip output_archive.zip
creator: Alise J Ponsero
